question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: Nevada and Rhode Island have very similar GDP levels.
options.C: The GDP gap between Nevada and Rhode Island might exceed that between Kentucky and Missouri.
options.D: All four states (Nevada, Rhode Island, Kentucky, Missouri) have nearly identical GDP levels.

explanation: Nevada (209.7$B) and Rhode Island (360.1$B) have a 150.4$B GDP difference, while Kentucky (399.9$B) and Missouri (437.7$B) have only a 37.8$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Nevada and Rhode Island into the same color bin (200-400$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while Kentucky and Missouri appear more different due to different color bins.
